For measuring/confirming dimensions in PDS, the best-supported archives found here are LRO LOLA altimetry (topography) and LRO Mini-RF derived global mosaics for regional context; PDS3 equivalents are available for both LOLA and Mini-RF mosaics.

candidates.title: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter LOLA Processed Data (RDR) Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Primary LRO topography archive (processed/derived). Use for elevation models to estimate pit depth and surrounding rim geometry.

candidates.title: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter LOLA RDR Gridded Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Ready-to-use gridded topographic products (DEMs/grids) derived from LOLA; convenient for measuring pit depth and local slopes.

candidates.title: LRO-L-LOLA-3-RDR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 version of LOLA processed (RDR) archive; use if your workflow expects PDS3 volumes.

candidates.title: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter LOLA Raw Data (EDR) Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Raw LOLA shots/profiles; useful if you want to recompute or custom-filter altimetry tracks across the pit area.

candidates.title: LRO-L-LOLA-2-EDR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 equivalent of LOLA raw (EDR) archive.

candidates.title: LRO Mini-RF Global Mosaic Archive Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Regional radar backscatter mosaics that can help place the pit within surrounding mare basalt units (context, not direct pit geometry at ~100 m scale).

candidates.title: LRO-L-MRFLRO-5-GLOBAL-MOSAIC-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 equivalent of the LRO Mini-RF global mosaic products.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
What is the overall description of the Mare Tranquillitatis Pit and what are the dimensions?
